report_2w.executive_summary: Canadian federal agencies must patch critical Cisco ASA and Firepower devices immediately—CCCS issued AL25-012 for three CVEs, and threat actors are deploying the FIRESTARTER backdoor that survives firmware updates on these same devices. CISA added four actively exploited vulnerabilities to its KEV catalog with a May 2026 federal deadline. The Bitwarden CLI npm package was compromised to steal developer credentials in a supply-chain attack, and ADT confirmed a breach by ShinyHunters following vishing attacks. Lazarus Group is actively targeting macOS users with ClickFix social engineering to deploy malware disguised as meeting invitations.

items.cia_impact: Not specified in advisory; device compromise likely affects all three

items.description: Historical vulnerability detected in automated scanning attempts

items.name: UNC6692
items.ttps: T1566.002 Phishing: Spearphishing Link, T1204 User Execution, T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter - Impersonates IT help desk via Microsoft Teams to deploy SNOW malware

items.name: Qilin (ransomware group)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service - Double extortion ransomware operations

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korean APT)
items.ttps: T1566.002 Phishing: Spearphishing Link, T1204.001 User Execution: Malicious Link, T1204.002 User Execution: Malicious File - Uses ClickFix attack to trick macOS users into fake meeting pages and executing malicious commands

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.ttps: T1566.004 Phishing: Spearphishing Voice (vishing), T1078 Valid Accounts, T1213 Data from Information Repositories - Used vishing to compromise Salesforce access and exfiltrate customer data including PII

items.name: UAT-4356 (Chinese state-aligned actors)
items.targets: Cisco Firepower and ASA devices, U.S. federal agencies
items.ttps: T1542.005 Pre-OS Boot: TFTP Boot, T1014 Rootkit, T1037 Boot or Logon Initialization Scripts - Deploys FIRESTARTER backdoor that establishes persistent foothold and survives security patches and firmware updates

items.name: incransom (ransomware group)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service - Ransomware operations with data leak site

items.name: Xyph0rix (affiliated with Defacer Indonesian Team)
items.ttps: T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application, T1078 Valid Accounts - Leaked alleged NASA database containing PII, credentials, and financial information

items.name: AiLock (ransomware group)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service - Claims exposure of phone numbers and physical addresses

items.name: Lamashtu (ransomware group)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service - Ransomware victim posted on leak site

items.name: Anubis (ransomware group)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service - Allegedly exfiltrated 4.1 TB of data affecting 37,500+ patients and 3,900 employees

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL25-012 on April 23, 2026, warning of critical vulnerabilities in Cisco ASA and FTD devices (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) requiring immediate patching. This is especially urgent given Chinese state-aligned actors are exploiting similar Cisco devices with the FIRESTARTER backdoor. Toronto police seized 'SMS blasters'—devices that impersonate legitimate cell towers to send mass phishing SMS—marking the first seizure of such cybercrime tools in Canada.
